title: Microsoft raises Xbox Series X|S prices by up to $150 worldwide
summary: The component crisis forces the company to raise the prices of its consoles, also eliminating the 2TB model.

title: Apple raises prices of Mac, iPad, and home devices due to RAM memory crisis
summary: The increase in NAND Flash and DRAM costs forces Apple to apply increases of up to $400 on some models.

title: Valve's Steam Machine faces criticism over price, performance, and RAM configuration
summary: Valve adjusts its performance claims to 4K, while retailers and competitors question the console's price and memory configuration.

title: IBM presents the first sub-1 nanometer chip technology with vertically stacked transistors
summary: The new Nanostack transistor architecture enables unprecedented miniaturization, paving the way for future processors.

title: Epic Games CEO criticizes Valve for 'irresponsible' tagging of games with AI
summary: Tim Sweeney calls Steam's decision to add tags on the use of artificial intelligence in games irresponsible.

title: ASUS restores TSME encryption on its AM5 motherboards after controversy with users
summary: The company launches a beta BIOS that recovers TSME memory encryption on the X870, B850, and X670 models.

title: AMD FSR 4.1 shows lower performance than FSR 3.1 on RDNA 3 cards
summary: Initial tests indicate a performance loss of up to 14%, although SDK 2.3 introduces improvements in ray regeneration.

title: AMD relaunches Ryzen 7 5800X3D with a 10th anniversary edition and speculators drive up its price
summary: The reissued processor appears with irregular inventory and sells for over $600 on the resale market.

title: Enshrouded leaves early access and announces its launch on PC and PS5 for October
summary: The popular survival and building game receives its version 1.0 after two years of early access development.

title: Grand Theft Auto VI faces censorship and plans a physical edition in December
summary: Rockstar deals with content cuts and prepares a disc launch for December this year.

title: AceMagic offers up to 20% discount on its Mini PCs with AMD Ryzen processors
summary: The company launches a temporary promotion with exclusive coupons for various Mini PC models.

title: Sony confirms 'significant' layoffs at Bungie
summary: PlayStation's parent company announces a reduction in staff at the studio responsible for Destiny.

title: Micron signs supply agreements worth $100 billion and warns that the RAM crisis has no end in sight
summary: The memory company says it doesn't know when the memory chip shortage will end.

title: Anthropic accuses Alibaba of using 25,000 fake accounts to illegally distill its Claude model
summary: The AI company detected 28.8 million fraudulent exchanges between April and June 2026.

title: Riot Vanguard removes its controversial permanent execution requirement
summary: The anti-cheat program now runs on demand, although it requires a strict Windows 11 security configuration.

title: Qualcomm presents its near-memory HBC architecture and the AI250 and AI350 accelerators
summary: The new technology promises 6 times more bandwidth per watt compared to HBM and 200 times more capacity than on-chip SRAM.

title: Qualcomm plans data center processors specific to China with limited AI accelerators
summary: The new Dragonfly line will comply with export thresholds by reducing AI capabilities.

title: Windows 10 extends its lifespan with free support until 2027
summary: Microsoft extends the operating system's compatibility beyond the initial end date.

title: NVIDIA updates its DLSS SDK to version 310.7.0 with support for DLSS Swapper
summary: The new version of the development kit allows swapping DLSS versions between games more easily.

title: Framework switches to ADATA XPG MARS 970 SSDs and warns about an upcoming CPU price increase
summary: The modular laptop company updates its recommended storage and anticipates increases in processor costs.
